questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given $(2+6+10+14+\ldots \ldots+2 a)+(2+6+10+14+\ldots \ldots .+2 b)=(2+6+10+14+\ldots \ldots .+2 c)$ where $\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c}=21$ and $\mathrm{a}\gt6$. Then $\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b}$ is equal to___
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 2(1+3+5+7+\ldots \ldots+a)+2(1+3+5+7+\ldots \ldots+b)=2(1+3+5+7+\ldots \ldots+c) \\ & \Rightarrow\left[2+\left(\frac{a+1}{2}-1\right) 2\right] \frac{a+1}{2}+\left[2+\left(\frac{b+1}{2}-1\right) 2\right] \frac{b+1}{2}=\left[2+\left(\frac{c+1}{2}-1\right) 2\right] \frac{c+1}{2}\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow(\mathrm{a}+1)^2+(\mathrm{b}+1)^2=(\mathrm{c}+1)^2$
$\because a\gt6$ and $a+b+c=21$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore a=7, b=5, c=9 \\ & \therefore a-b=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=x^2+3 x+1$ and $g(x)=x+1$. If $f(x)+\lambda g(x)>-10 \forall x \in R$ then find the sum of all possible positive integral values of $\lambda$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x^2+3 x+1+\lambda(x+1)>-10 \\
& x^2+(3+\lambda) x+11+\lambda>0 \forall x \in R \\
& D < 0 \Rightarrow(\lambda+3)^2-4(11+\lambda) < 0 \\
& \lambda^2+6 \lambda+9-44-4 \lambda < 0 \\
& \Rightarrow \lambda^2+2 \lambda-35 < 0 \\
& \Rightarrow \lambda \in(-7,5)
\end{aligned}
$$

Required sum $=1+2+3+4=10$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f(x)=x^2-2 p x+p^2-1$, where $p \in R-\{-1,1\}$.
If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are distinct real roots of the equation $f(x)=0$ such that $\left|\frac{\alpha^2+\beta^2+3 \alpha \beta}{\alpha \beta}\right| \leq 5$, then set of values of $p \in[a, b]$. The value of $\left[2\left(a^2+b^2\right)\right]$ is:
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \quad x^2-2 p x+p^2-1=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x=p \pm 1 \\ & \left|\frac{\alpha^2+\beta^2}{\alpha \beta}+3\right| \leq 5 \Rightarrow-8 \leq \frac{\alpha^2+\beta^2}{\alpha \beta} \leq 2 \Rightarrow-8 \leq \frac{\alpha^2+\beta^2}{\alpha \beta} \leq 2 \Rightarrow-8 \leq \frac{2\left(p^2+1\right)}{p^2-1} \leq 2 \\ & \quad-4 \leq \frac{p^2+1}{p^2-1} \leq 1 \Rightarrow-4 \leq 1+\frac{2}{p^2-1} \leq 1 \Rightarrow-5 \leq \frac{2}{p^2-1} < 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad p^2-1 \leq \frac{-2}{5} \Rightarrow p^2 \leq \frac{3}{5} \Rightarrow p \in\left[-\sqrt{\frac{3}{5}}, \sqrt{\frac{3}{5}}\right] \equiv[a, b] \\ & \therefore \quad\left[2\left(a^2+b^2\right)\right]=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the greatest value of $\frac{x^2-x+c}{x^2+x+c}$ is $\frac{5}{3}$, then find the value of $c$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} y & =\frac{x^2-x+c}{x^2+x+c} \leq 1-\frac{2}{1-2 \sqrt{c}}=\frac{5}{3} \\ \Rightarrow \quad c & =4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f$ be monic cubic polynomial such that $f(1)=1^4-1, f(2)=2^4-2$ and $f(3)=3^4-3$. If $f(4)=N$, then find the number of prime factors of $N$.
questions.solution:
$$
f(x)=(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)(c-x)+x^4-x
$$
Coefficient of $x^3=1$
$$
\therefore \quad c+1+2+3=1 \Rightarrow c=-5
$$
$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)(-5-x)+x^4-x \\ & f(4)=3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1(-9)+4^4-4=198=2 \times 3^2 \times 11\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $a, b, c$ and $d$ be four roots of the equation $x^4-10 x^3+37 x^2-60 x+32=0$.
If $\frac{1}{a^2-5 a+10}+\frac{1}{b^2-5 b+10}+\frac{1}{c^2-5 c+10}+\frac{1}{d^2-5 d+10}=\frac{p}{q}$, where $p$ and $q$ are co-prime numbers, then find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \left(x^2-5 x+10\right)\left(x^2-5 x+2\right)+12=0 \\ & \left(x^2-5 x+10\right)\left(x^2-5 x+10-8\right)+12=0 \\ & \left(x^2-5 x+10\right)^2-8\left(x^2-5 x+10\right)+12=0 \\ & x^2-5 x+10=2,6 \\ & \therefore \sum \frac{1}{a^2-5 a+10}=\frac{2}{2}+\frac{2}{6}=\frac{4}{3}=\frac{p}{q} \\ & \therefore \quad p+q=7\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ are the roots of the equation $a x^2+b x+c=0$ where $\beta=4 \alpha(\alpha>0)$. If $3 a=2(c-b)$ and $S=\sum_{r=0}^{\infty} \beta\left(\alpha^r\right)$, then find the value of $3 S$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 3=2\left(\frac{c}{a}-\frac{b}{a}\right) \\ & 3=2\left(4 \alpha^2+5 \alpha\right) \Rightarrow 8 \alpha^2+10 \alpha-3=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 8 \alpha^2+12 \alpha-2 \alpha-3=0 \Rightarrow(2 \alpha+3)(4 \alpha-1)=0 \\ & \alpha=\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow \beta=4 \alpha=1 \\ & S=\frac{\beta}{1-\alpha}=\frac{1}{1-\frac{1}{4}}=\frac{4}{3} \Rightarrow 3 S=4 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the number of integral values of $k$ for which $e^{\lambda^2-2 \lambda+1+\ln 3}$ and $e^{-\left(\lambda^2-2 \lambda+1\right)+\ln 2}$, where $\lambda \in R-\{1\}$ are the roots of the equation $x^2-(3 k+1) x+3 k^2-k+2=0$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text { Product of roots } & =3 k^2-k+2=e^{\ln 3+\ln 2}=6 \\
3 k^2-k-4 & =0 \\
\Rightarrow \quad 3 k^2-4 k+3 k-4 & =0 \\
(k+1)(3 k-4) & =0 \\
k & =-1, k=\frac{4}{3} \\
\text { Sum of roots } & =3 k+1=3 \cdot e^{\lambda^2-2 \lambda+1}+2 \cdot e^{-\left(\lambda^2-2 \lambda+1\right)}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\because k=-1$ not satisfy given relation
So, number of integral value of $k$ is zero.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $P$ be the sum of integral values of $b$ in the interval $[1,20]$ for which the equation $4^x+(1-b) 2^{x+1}+b=0$ has roots of apposite sign, then the value of $\frac{p}{51}$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Let $2^{\mathrm{x}}=\mathrm{t}($ for $\mathrm{x} \lt 0, \mathrm{t} \lt 1$ and for $\mathrm{x}\gt0, \mathrm{t}\gt1)$
Now $g(t)=t^2+2(1-b) t+b=0$
Unity must be between the roots of this equation $g(1) \lt 0$
$1+2(1-b)+b \lt 0 \Rightarrow b\gt3$
Possible values of b in [1, 20] are $4,5,6, \ldots . .19,20$
So $p=204=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the quadratic equation $x^2+(2-\tan \theta) x-(1+\tan \theta)=0$ has two integral roots, then sum of all possible values of $\theta$ in the interval $(0,2 \pi)$ is $k \pi$, then the value of $k$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{x}^2+(2-\tan \theta) \mathrm{x}-(1+\tan \theta)=0 \\
& \alpha+\beta=\tan \theta-2....(i)\\
& \alpha \beta=-\tan \theta-1
\end{aligned}$
From equation (1) $+(2)$
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha+\beta+\alpha \beta=-3 \\
& (\alpha+1)(\beta+1)=-2
\end{aligned}$
Hence either $\alpha+1=-2$ and $\beta+1=1$
then $\alpha=-3, \beta=0$
or $\alpha+1=-1$ and $\beta+1=2$
$\alpha=-2$ and $\beta=1$
If $\alpha=-3, \beta=0$ then $\tan \theta=-1$
$\Rightarrow \alpha=\frac{3 \pi}{4}, \frac{7 \pi}{4}$
If $\alpha=-2, \beta=1$ then $\tan \theta=1$
$\Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{5 \pi}{4}$
Sum $=\frac{3 \pi}{4}+\frac{7 \pi}{4}+\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{5 \pi}{4}=4 \pi=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the number of irrational solutions of the equation $\sqrt{x^2+\sqrt{x^2+11}}+\sqrt{x^2-\sqrt{x^2+11}}=4$
questions.solution:
Put $\sqrt{x^2+11}=1$ so that $\sqrt{x^2+\sqrt{x^2+11}}+\sqrt{x^2-\sqrt{x^2+11}}=4$ can be written as $\sqrt{t^2+1-11}+\sqrt{t^2-1-11}$
We have also have the identity
$\left(t^2+t-11\right)-\left(t^2-t-11\right)=2 t$   ...(2)
Dividing (1) by (2), we get
$\sqrt{t^2+t-11}-\sqrt{t^2-1-11}=\frac{t}{2}$   ...(3)
Adding (2) and (3), we get
$\begin{aligned}
& 2\left(\sqrt{t^2+t-11}\right)=4+\frac{t}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow t^2+t-11=4+t+\frac{t^2}{16} \Rightarrow t=4 \Rightarrow x= \pm \sqrt{5}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If roots $x_1$ and $x_2$ of the equation $x^2-4 a x+1=0$ satisfy $x_1 \geq a$ and $x_2 \geq 0$, then a $\in\left[\frac{1}{k}, x\right), k$ must be $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$D \geq 0 \Rightarrow a^2 \geq \frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow a \leq-\frac{1}{2}$ or $a \geq \frac{1}{2}$
Case -I: If $\mathrm{a} \leq-\frac{1}{2}$, then a is negative and $x_1 \geq a, x_2 \geq 0$
$\Rightarrow$ atleast one root must be positive
$\Rightarrow$ either both roots are positive or exactly one root is positive.
Now $\mathrm{x}^2-4 \mathrm{ax}+1=0$. can not have both roots positive. Since sum of the roots $=4 \mathrm{a} \lt 0$
Again exactly one root can not be positive since product $\gt0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{a} \leq-\frac{1}{2}$ is not possible
Case-II: if $a \geq \frac{1}{2}$, then $x_1 \geq-\frac{1}{2}, x_2 \geq 0$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{x}_1+\mathrm{x}_2 \geq 0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{ta} \geq 0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{a} \geq \frac{1}{2}$
So $\mathrm{a} \in\left[\frac{1}{2}, \infty\right) \Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If 1 lies between the roots of the equation $p^2-m p+1=0$ and $[x]$ denotes the greatest integer function then the value of $\left[\left(\frac{4|x|}{|x|^2+16}\right)^m\right]$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(p)=p^2-m p+1 \because 1 \text { lies in between roots } \\ & \therefore \Delta\gt0, f(1) \lt 0 \\ & \therefore m^2-4\gt0,1-m+1 \lt 0 \\ & m \lt -2 \text { or } m\gt2 \text { and } m\gt2 \\ & A M \geq G . M \\ & \frac{|x|^2+16}{2} \geq 4|x| .0 \leq \frac{4|x|}{|x|^2+16} \leq \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow 0 \lt \left(\frac{4|x|}{|x|^2+16}\right)^m \lt 1 \\ & \therefore\left[\left(\frac{4|x|}{|x|^2+16}\right)^m\right]=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=x^2+\alpha x+\beta$ where $\alpha, \beta \in R$ and $f(f(x))=0$ has 2 roots 1 and 2 . Find the value of $2|f(0)|$.
questions.solution:
Given,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(f(1))=0 \Rightarrow f(1+\alpha+\beta)=0 \\
& f(f(2))=0 \Rightarrow f(4+2 \alpha+\beta)=0
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, roots of $f(x)$ are $\alpha+\beta+1$ and $2 \alpha+\beta+4$
Now, sum of roots $=3 \alpha+2 \beta+5=-\alpha$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad 4 \alpha+2 \beta=-5
$$...(1)

Now, product of roots $=(\alpha+\beta+1)(2 \alpha+\beta+4)=\beta$
$$
(\alpha+\beta+1) \frac{3}{2}=\beta
$$...(2)

From Eqns. (1) and (2), $b=f(0)=\frac{-3}{2}$
Hence, $2|f(0)|=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\alpha$ is a real root of the equation $x^5-x^3+x-2=0$, then find the value of $\left[\alpha^6\right]$. [Note: [ ] ] denotes the greatest integer function.]
questions.solution:
Let
$$
\begin{aligned}
f(x) & =x^5-x^3+x-2 \\
f^{\prime}(x) & =5 x^4-3 x^2+1>0 \forall x \in R
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore f(x)$ is increasing $\Rightarrow$ only one real root.
$$
f(1)=-1, f(2)=24 \Rightarrow 1 < \alpha < 2
$$

Since, $\alpha$ is a root of $x^5-x^3+x-2=0$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\alpha^5-\alpha^3+\alpha & =2 \\
\alpha^4-\alpha^2+1 & =\frac{2}{\alpha} \\
\left(\alpha^2+1\right)\left(\alpha^4-\alpha^2+1\right) & =\frac{2}{\alpha}\left(\alpha^2+1\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{gathered}
\alpha^6+1=2 \alpha+\frac{2}{\alpha} \Rightarrow \alpha^6=2 \alpha+\frac{2}{\alpha}-1 \\
g(\alpha)=2 \alpha+\frac{2}{\alpha}-1 \\
g^{\prime}(\alpha)=2-\frac{2}{\alpha^2}=\frac{2}{\alpha^2}\left(\alpha^2-1\right)=\frac{2}{\alpha^2}(\alpha-1)(\alpha+1)
\end{gathered}
$$
$g$ is increasing for $\alpha>1$.
$$
\begin{aligned}
g(1) & < g(\alpha) < g(2) \\
3 & < g(\alpha) < 4 \\
3 & < \alpha^6 < 4 \\
{\left[\alpha^6\right] } & =3
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \quad\left[\alpha^6\right]=3
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $\cos ^4 \theta+\alpha$ and $\sin ^4 \theta+\alpha$ are the roots of the equation $x^2+b(2 x+1)=0$ and $\cos ^2 \theta+\beta$ and $\sin ^2 \theta+\beta$ are the roots of the equation $x^2+4 x+2=0$, then find the value of $b$ where $b \in N$.
[Note: $\theta$ can be non-real number also.]
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \cos ^2 \theta+\sin ^2 \theta+2 \beta & =-4 \\ \beta & =\frac{-5}{2} \\ \left(\cos ^2 \theta+\beta\right)\left(\sin ^2 \theta+\beta\right) & =2 \Rightarrow \sin ^2 2 \theta=-7 \\ \cos ^4 \theta+\sin ^4 \theta+2 \alpha & =-2 b \\ 1-\frac{\sin ^2 2 \theta}{2}+2 \alpha & =-2 b\end{aligned}$
$9+4 \alpha=-4 b$ ...(1)
$$
\begin{aligned}
\left(\cos ^4 \theta+\alpha\right)\left(\sin ^4 \theta+\alpha\right) & =b \\
\frac{\sin ^4 2 \theta}{10}+\frac{9 \alpha}{2}+\alpha^2 & =b \\
(4 \alpha+5)(4 \alpha+17) & =0
\end{aligned}
$$

If
$$
\alpha=\frac{-5}{4} \Rightarrow b=-1 \text { (rejected) }
$$

If
$$
\alpha=\frac{-17}{4} \Rightarrow b=2
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If set of values of 'a' for which $f(x)=a x^2-(3+2 a) x+6, a \neq 0$ is positive for exactly three distinct negative integral values of $x$ is $(c, d]$ then the value of $\left(c^2+16 d^2\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} f(x) & =a x^2-(3+2 a) x+6 \\ & =(a x-3)(x-2) \end{aligned}$ Here, roots of the equation $f(x)=0$ are 2 and $\frac{3}{a}$, and $f(0)=6$ $f(x)$ should be positive for exactly three negative integral values of $x$. Therefore, graph of $f(x)$ must be a downward parabola passing through $x=2$ and $x=\frac{3}{a}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { and }-4 \leq \frac{3}{a} \lt -3 \\ & \therefore \quad a \in\left(-1,-\frac{3}{4}\right] \\ & \therefore \quad c=-1, d=-\frac{3}{4} \\ & \Rightarrow c^2+16 d^2=1+16 \times \frac{9}{16}=1+9=10\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ and $\gamma$ be three distinct real roots of the equation $x(3 x+2)^2+2=(a+12+9 x) x^2-b x+c$ where $a, b$, $c \in R$. If every solution of the inequality $(x-a)^2(4 x+b)(x-c) \lt 0$ is also solution of the inequality $3 x^2+p x+$ $p^2+6 p \lt 0$ then find number of integral values of ' $p$ '
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x(3 x+2)^2+2=(a+12+9 x) x^2-b x+c \\ & \Rightarrow 4 x+2=a x^2-b x+c \end{aligned}$ which is quadratic in x but it satisfies by $\alpha, \beta$ and $\gamma$ so it is an identity. $\therefore \mathrm{a}=0, \mathrm{~b}=-4, \mathrm{c}=2$
$\begin{aligned} & x^2(4 x-4)(x-2) \lt 0 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2(x-1)(x-2) \lt 0 \\ & x \in(1,2) \\ & 3 x^2+p x+p^2+6 p \lt 0 \text { satisfy by every } x \in(1,2) \\ & f(1) \leq 0 \text { and } f(2) \leq 0 \\ & p^2+7 p+3 \leq 0 \\ & \text { and } p^2+8 p+12 \leq 0 \\ & p \in\left[\frac{-7-\sqrt{37}}{2}, \frac{-7+\sqrt{37}}{2}\right] \\ & (p+2)(p+6) \leq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow p \in[-6,-2] \\ & p \in[-6.5 \ldots,-0 \ldots]-6,-5,-4,-3,-2 \end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad$ Number of integers are 5
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\left\{y \in R \mid \forall x \in R, y=\frac{x^2+m x+1}{x^2-x+1} \subset[-3,2]\right\}$, then find the number of integral values of $m$
questions.solution:
We have $-3 \leq \frac{x^2+m x+1}{x^2-x+1} \leq 2 \forall x \in R$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { So, } x^2+m x+1 \leq 2 x^2-2 x+2 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2-(m+2)^2 x+1 \geq 0 \forall x \in R \\ & \therefore \quad(m+2)^2-4 \leq 0 \end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow m(m+4) \leq 0$ .....(1)
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Again }-3 \leq \frac{x^2+m x+1}{x^2-x+1} \\ & \quad-3 x^2+3 x-3 \leq x^2+m x+1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 4 x^2+(m-3) x+4 \geq 0 \forall x \in R \\ & \therefore \quad(m-3)^2-64 \leq 0\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow(m-11)(m+5) \leq 0$ .....(2) $\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & (1) \cap(2) \Rightarrow m \in[-4,0] \\ \Rightarrow & \text { number of integers }=5\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=x^2+a x+b$. If $\forall x \in R$, there exist a real value of $y$ such that $f(y)=f(x)+y$, then find the maximum value of $100 a$
questions.solution:
Given, $f(y)=f(x)+y$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow y^2+a y+b=x^2+a x+b+y \\
& \Rightarrow y^2+y(a-1)-x^2-a x=0
\end{aligned}$

As, $y \in R$, so $D \geq 0 \quad \forall x \in R$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad(a-1)^2+4\left(x^2+a x\right) \geq 0 \quad \forall x \in R \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 4 x^2+4 a x+a^2-2 a+1 \geq 0 \quad \forall x \in R
\end{aligned}$

Now, $\quad \mathrm{D} \leq 0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow 16 a^2-16\left(a^2-2 a+1\right) \leq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 2 a-1 \leq 0 \Rightarrow a \leq \frac{1}{2}
\end{aligned}$

So, $a_{\max .}=\frac{1}{2}$
Hence, maximum value of $100 \mathrm{a}=50$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $a_1, a_2, \ldots \ldots, a_n$ be the roots of the polynomial $\sum_{k=1}^n k x^k=0$. If $\sum_{k=1}^n \frac{1}{\left(1-a_k\right)^2}=-13$. Find $n$.
questions.solution:
Let
$$
\begin{aligned}
P(x) & =\sum_{k=1}^n k x^k \equiv n\left(x-a_1\right)\left(x-a_2\right) \ldots \ldots\left(x-a_n\right) \\
\ln P(x) & =\ln n+\sum_{i=1}^n \ln \left(x-a_i\right)
\end{aligned}
$$

Differentiate two times and put $x=1$, we get
$$
\frac{P(1) P^{\prime \prime}(1)-\left(P^{\prime}(1)\right)^2}{(P(1))^2}=\sum_{k=1}^n \frac{1}{\left(1-a_k\right)^2}=13
$$

Now,
$$
P(x)=\sum_{k=1}^n k x^k
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
P(1) & =\sum_{k=1}^n k=\frac{n(n+1)}{2} \\
P^{\prime}(1) & =\sum_{k=1}^n k^2=\frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6} \\
P^{\prime \prime}(1)=\sum_{k=1}^n k^2(k-1) & =\sum n^3-\sum n^2=\left(\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right)^2-\frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6}
\end{aligned}
$$

On putting these values we get $n=22$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $z$ ( $z \in$ complex number) be one of the roots of the equation $x^2-\left(\log _2 \alpha-\log _2 \beta\right) x+\cos \alpha-\sin \beta=0$. If the harmonic mean of the roots is 2 and $|z|=1$, find the sum of all values of $\beta$ in degrees when $0 < \beta < 360^{\circ}$.
questions.solution:
Let the roots be $a-i b, a+i b$, then $a^2+b^2=1$ (given $\left.|z|=1\right)$
Also,
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text { H.M. } & =2 \\
a^2+b^2 & =2 a
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore \quad a=\frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore$ Quadratic equation $x^2-x(2 a)+a^2+b^2=0$
$x^2-x+1=0$ ...(1)
$x^2-\log _2\left(\frac{\alpha}{\beta}\right)+\cos \alpha-\sin \beta=0$ ...(2)
On comparing equations (1) and (2), we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \log _2\left(\frac{\alpha}{\beta}\right) & =1 \quad \text { and } \quad \cos \alpha-\sin \beta=1 \\
\alpha & =2 \beta \text { in }(\cos \alpha-\sin \beta=1) \\
\cos 2 \beta-\sin \beta & =1 \\
2 \sin ^2 \beta+\sin \beta & =0 \\
\sin \beta & =\frac{-1}{2} \text { or } \sin \beta=0 \\
\quad \beta & =\frac{7 \pi}{6}=210^{\circ} \text { or } 330^{\circ} \text { or } 180^{\circ} \\
\therefore \quad \text { Sum } & =720
\end{aligned}
$$

questions.q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ are the roots of the quadratic equation $2 x^2-5 x+1=0$. If $S_n=(\alpha)^{2 n}+(\beta)^{2 n}$ then find the value of $\frac{4 S_{2021}+S_{2019}}{S_{2020}}$.
questions.solution:
Given $\alpha, \beta$ are the roots of the quadratic equation $2 x^2-5 x+1=0$
Let us find an equation with roots $\alpha^2$ and $\beta^2$, let $y=x^2$, so $x=\sqrt{y}$
$$
2 y-5 \sqrt{y}+1=0 \Rightarrow 2 y+1=5 \sqrt{y} \Rightarrow 4 y^2+4 y+1=25 y \Rightarrow 4 y^2-21 y+1=0\left\langle{ }_d^c\right.
$$

Put
$$
\alpha^2=c \text { and } \beta^2=d
$$

Now,
$$
S_n=(c)^n+(d)^n
$$

Consider
$$
\begin{aligned}
4 S_{2021}+S_{2019} & =4\left(c^{2021}+d^{2021}\right)+c^{2019}+d^{2019} \\
& =c^{2019}\left(4 c^2+1\right)+d^{2019}\left(4 d^2+1\right) \\
& =c^{2019}(21 c)+d^{2019}(21 d) \\
& =21 S_{2020}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence,
$$
\frac{4 S_{2021}+S_{2019}}{S_{2020}}=21
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If the equation $x^2+2 a x+a=\sqrt{a^2+x-\frac{1}{16}}-\frac{1}{16}$ has no real roots, then the range of $a$ is $(p, q)$ where $p$ and $q$ are rational numbers. If $p^2+q^2=\frac{c}{d}$ (where $c$ and $d$ are co-prime) then find the value of $\sqrt{4 c^2-d^2}$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
x^2+2 a x+a & =\sqrt{a^2+x-\frac{1}{16}}-\frac{1}{16} \\
(x+a)^2+a-a^2+\frac{1}{16} & =\sqrt{(x+a)-\left(a-a^2+\frac{1}{16}\right)}
\end{aligned}
$$

Let
$$
x+a=y \text { and } a-a^2+\frac{1}{16}=p
$$

Then
$$
y^2+p=\sqrt{y-p} \equiv f(x)=f^{-1}(x)
$$
$f(x)=x$ has no real root $\Rightarrow y^2+p=y$ has no real root
$$
\Rightarrow
$$
$$
\begin{array}{rr}
D < 0 \Rightarrow p>\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow a-a^2+\frac{1}{16}>\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow a \in\left(\frac{1}{4}, \frac{3}{4}\right) \\
\therefore & \frac{c}{d}=\frac{5}{8} \Rightarrow \sqrt{4 c^2-d^2}=\sqrt{ }
\end{array}
$$
$$
\frac{c}{d}=\frac{5}{8} \Rightarrow \sqrt{4 c^2-d^2}=\sqrt{100-64}=6
$$

questions.q: Let $a, b$ be the roots of the quadratic equation $x^2-(k-2) c x-(k-1) d=0$ and $c, d$ be the roots of the quadratic equation $x^2-(k-2) a x-(k-1) b=0$. If $a, b, c$ and $d$ are distinct, non-zero real numbers such that $a+b+c+d=100$, then find the integral value of $k$.

questions.q:
Let $f$ be a polynomial function of degree 3 satisfying $f(1)=3, f(2)=5$ and $f(3)=7$. If product of the roots of the equation $(f(x))^2+4 x f(x)+3 x^2=0$ is 4 and the sum of all possible values of $f(4)$ is $k$ then find $[k]$.
[Note: $[y]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $y$.]
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
f(x) & =a(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)+2 x+1 \\
(f(x))^2+4 x f(x)+3 x^2 & =0
\end{aligned}
$$

Product of the roots, $\quad \frac{(1-6 a)^2}{a^2}=4 \Rightarrow 1-6 a= \pm 2 a\left\langle\begin{array}{l}a=1 / 8 \\ a=1 / 4\end{array}\right.$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \\ & f(4)=6 a+9 < { }_{a=1 / 4}^{n=1 / 8}-\frac{3}{4}+9 \\ & k=20+\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow[k]=20 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Number of real values of $\lambda$ such that $\left(\lambda^2-4 \lambda+3\right) x^2+\left(\lambda^2-5 \lambda+6\right) x+\left(\lambda^2-9\right)=0$ has more than 2 roots is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lambda^2-4 \lambda+3=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=1,3 \\ & \lambda^2-5 \lambda+6=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=2,3 \\ & \lambda^2-9=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=3,-3 \\ & \therefore \quad \lambda=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If sum of the roots of the equation $2 \ln \left(4^x-2\right)=\ln 8+\ln \left(4^x-\frac{31}{8}\right)$ lies between two consecutive natural numbers $a$ and $b$, then find the value of $(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \ln \left(4^x-2\right)^2=\ln \left(8\left(4^x-\frac{31}{8}\right)\right) \\
& \left(4^x-2\right)^2=8 \cdot 4^x-31 \\
& 4^x=t \\
& (t-2)^2=8 t-31 \\
& t^2-12 t+35=0 \Rightarrow t=5,7 \\
& 4^x=5,7 \Rightarrow \quad x=\log _4 5, \log _4 7 \\
& \therefore \text { Sum of the roots }=\log _4 5+\log _4 7=\log _4 35 \in(2,3) \equiv(a, b) \\
& \therefore \quad a+b=5 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$

Let

questions.q: Let $f(x)=x^2-2 p x+3 p^2-5$ and $g(x)=-x^2+2 p x+2 p-3 q, p, q \in R$. If $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ do not intersects at two distinct points $\forall p \in R$, then find the least value of $q$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & x^2-2 p x+3 p^2-5=-x^2+2 p x+2 p-3 q \\ & 2 x^2-4 p x+3 p^2-2 p+3 q-5=0 \\ & D \leq 0 \\ & 2+6 p^2-4 \cdot 2\left(3 p^2-2 p+3 q-5\right) \leq 0 \\ &-p^2+2 p-3 q+5 \leq 0 \\ & p^2-2 p+3 q-5 \geq 0 \forall p \in R \\ & D \leq 0 \quad 4-4(3 q-5) \leq 0 \Rightarrow q \geq 2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If $8 \alpha^3+\beta^3-\gamma^3+6 \alpha \beta \gamma=0$ and $\alpha^2+3 \gamma=2 \beta$ where $\alpha, \beta, \gamma \in R$ and $\beta+\gamma \neq 0$, then find the largest integral value of $\gamma$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& (2 \alpha)^3+\beta^3+(-\gamma)^3=3(2 \alpha)(\beta)(-\gamma) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 \alpha+\beta-\gamma=0 \Rightarrow \beta=\gamma-2 \alpha \\
& 2 \alpha=\beta=-\gamma \Rightarrow \beta+\gamma=0 \\
& \{\text { But } \beta+\gamma \neq 0\} \\
& \alpha^2+3 \gamma=2(\gamma-2 \alpha) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \alpha^2+4 \alpha+\gamma=0 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
or

For $\alpha$ to be real, $D \geq 0$
$$
16-4 \gamma \geq 0 \Rightarrow \gamma \leq 4
$$

questions.q: Let $\sin ^2 \theta$ and $\tan ^2 \theta$ are roots of the quadratic equation $a x^2+b x+c=0$ and $\frac{b^2-c^2}{a c}=\lambda$. If solution set of the inequality $\log _\lambda(8 \sin x) < 1$ in $(0,2 \pi)$ is $(\alpha, \beta) \cup(\gamma, \delta)$, then find the value of $\left(\frac{\gamma}{\beta}+\frac{\alpha}{\delta}\right)$.
questions.solution:
$\sin ^2 \theta+\tan ^2 \theta=-b / a$ ...(1)
$\sin ^2 \theta \cdot \tan ^2 \theta=c / a$ ...(2)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Now, Eqn. (1) }{ }^2 \text { Eqn. (2) } \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{b^2-c^2}{a^2}=\left(\sin ^2 \theta+\tan ^2 \theta\right)^2-\left(\sin ^2 \theta \cdot \tan ^2 \theta\right)^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{b^2-c^2}{a^2}=\left(\sin ^2 \theta+\tan ^2 \theta+\sin ^2 \theta \cdot \tan ^2 \theta\right)\left(\sin ^2 \theta+\tan ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 \theta \cdot \tan ^2 \theta\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{b^2-c^2}{a^2}=\left(2 \tan ^2 \theta\right)\left(2 \sin ^2 \theta\right)=\frac{4 c}{a} \\
& \therefore \frac{b^2-c^2}{a c}=4 \Rightarrow \lambda=4
\end{aligned}
$$

Now, $\log _4(8 \sin x) < 1$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 0 < \sin x < 1 / 2 \\
& x \in(0, \pi / 6) \cup(5 \pi / 6, \pi) \equiv(\alpha, \beta) \cup(\gamma, \delta) \\
& \gamma+\alpha
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \quad \frac{\gamma}{\beta}+\frac{\alpha}{\delta}=5+0=5
$$

questions.q: A quadratic equation with integral coefficients has two prime numbers as its roots. If the sum of the coefficients of the equation is prime, then the sum of the roots is ______ .
questions.solution:
Let the equation be $K(x-\alpha)(x-\beta)=0$
$\therefore$ Sum of coefficient $=\mathrm{K}(\alpha-1)(\beta-1)$ is prime
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{K}$ is 1 and $\alpha-1$ is 1
$\Rightarrow$ One root is 2 and $\beta-1$ is 2
Hence roots are 2 and 3.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If $x^4+3 \cos \left(a x^2+b x+c\right)=2\left(x^2-2\right)$ has two solutions with $a, b, c \in(2,5)$ then the maximum value of $\frac{\mathrm{ac}}{\mathrm{b}^2}$ is _______
questions.solution:
$\Rightarrow\left(x^2-1\right)^2=-3\left\{1+\cos \left(a x^2+b x+c\right)\right\}$ solution exists
when L.H.S. $=$ R.H.S. $=0$
$\Rightarrow x= \pm 1$ and $\cos \left(a x^2+b x+c\right)=-1$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { When } x=1, x=-1 \\ & \Rightarrow a+b+c=3 \pi, a-b+c=\pi\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow b=\pi$ and $a+c=2 \pi$
